title: HK protocol pacifist package
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Device |image=[[File:HKprotocolpacifistpackage.png]] |name=HK protocol pacifist package |manufacturer= |model= |type= |culture= |creators= |created=By [[3951 BBY]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', which features the HK protocol pacifist package, to [[3951 BBY]]. |modifier= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners=*[[Geeda (Nar Shaddaa)|Geeda]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' *[[Meetra Surik]] {{C|Installed in [[HK-47]]}} |locations=[[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' |cost=204 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] {{C|Discounted rate at Geeda's store on Nar Shaddaa}} |value= |structure= |size= |material= |length= |width= |height= |weight= |protection= |capacity= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose=*Removal of [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] protocols in [[HK-series assassin droid]]s *Addition of pacification protocols *Suppression of prior memories |heritage= |affiliation= }} {{Dialogue|HK-47|Statement: I am fulfilling my primary function, which is to facilitate communication between species and put an end to hostilities. Greeting: Oh, hello, little T3 unit. I am HK-47, protocol and translation droid.|T3-M4|Dee… deet?|HK-47|Answer: Of course I am all right. Why wouldn't I be? I respect all life. All life is connected and should be nurtured.|Meetra Surik|That does it. That package is coming out of there.|attr=HK-47, after Meetra Surik installs the HK protocol pacifist package|src=}} The '''HK protocol pacifist package''' was a module for [[HK-series assassin droid]]s, created sometime by [[3951 BBY]]. Designed to be downloaded to the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid's]] behavior core, the device inhibited the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] routines and forced the [[Death/Legends|killing]] machine to function as a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]]. The module also suppressed the droid's memories of its former self and instilled an interest in education, including the learning of new [[Language/Legends|languages]]. When installed, the HK protocol pacifist package prevented the droid from creating violence and destruction, instead exhibiting a peaceful, pacifistic personality and expressing care for all living things. ==Description== {{Quote|Musing: I think perhaps I would enjoy learning a new language. Or watching an informative holovid.''"
"''I think I flipped the wrong switch. Or the right one.|HK-47 and Meetra Surik witness the effects of installing the HK protocol pacifist package|}} The HK protocol pacifist package was designed for use with the [[HK-series assassin droid]] to deactivate the [[Assassin droid/Legends|droid's]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] routines and add pacification [[Programmer/Legends|programming]] without the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] knowledge. Designed to be downloaded onto the droid's behavior core, the module could be installed by even the least technologically-savvy being. The package also worked to suppress the droid's memories; when asked to participate in combat or [[Death/Legends|kill]] another being, the droid expressed shock and dismay at the concept and recalled none of its prior behavior as an assassin. Now acting as a [[Translator/Legends|translator]] and [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol unit]], the droid became interested in teaching itself by means of educational activities—a desire instilled by the package's installation—including learning new [[Language/Legends|languages]] and watching informative [[Holovid/Legends|holovids]]. However, all of the functionality changes were not permanent: The package could be uninstalled, thereby returning an HK-series droid to its former self. The droid would maintain memory of the module's former presence and could maintain some understanding of the motivation for resolving conflicts peacefully, even void of the package's influence. ==History== {{Quote|Statement: Master, please, I beg you. Never install anything again without checking it in the diagnostics bay first. That was a close one—I almost surrendered completely to peace and pacifism—how repugnant. Conclusion: Still there was a brief moment where I felt like I almost understood why some meatbags choose peace and friendship over a high-powered blaster carbine. Query: Now, are there any other horrors you wish to try and insert in my system, or is your electronic butchery done?|HK-47 chastises Meetra Surik for installing the HK protocol pacifist package|}} The HK protocol pacifist package was created sometime by [[3951 BBY]]. It was designed to restrict the assassination protocols of HK-series assassin droids and effectively turn an affected unit into a pacifistic protocol droid. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}} [[File:RespectYourElders-K2.png|thumb|right|200px|The HK protocol pacifist package could be installed into an HK-series assassin droid, such as HK-47.]] During the [[Dark Wars]]—a series a conflicts between the remnants of the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], the [[Sith Triumvirate]], and fractured [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] forces—in 3951 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], an exiled [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Meetra Surik]], assisted [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Geeda (Nar Shaddaa)|Geeda]] in the formation of [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade routes]] between the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] and [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon's]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]]. As a result, Geeda came into possession of an HK protocol pacifist package, which she stocked in her storefront on the moon [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. Surik eventually purchased the device at a discounted rate of 204 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and later downloaded it into the behavior core of one of her companions, the assassin [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[HK-47]], as part of the Exile's attempts to upgrade the droid during her campaign. The installed module had an immediate impact on the droid's demeanor. Upon Surik's request to remain vigilant, HK-47 vehemently opposed any violent activity and instead showed a desire to learn. HK-47's completely reversed personality drew concern from Surik and her other companions, including [[T3-series utility droid|utility droid]] [[T3-M4]], and Surik removed the HK protocol pacifist package. Restored to his original self, HK-47 expressed disgust at the change forced upon him and requested that any further upgrades be run through a diagnostics bay before their installation. However, the droid begrudgingly admitted that the module had instilled some slight insight to the desire for peaceful resolutions. {{Endgame}} ==Behind the scenes== The HK protocol pacifist package appeared in the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. It featured in a brief, optional conversation that rewarded experience to players and a permanent stat boost to HK-47. An update for the [[Wikipedia:Steam (software)|Steam]] version of ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'', released concurrently with the game's Steam release on [[July 21]], [[2015]], added an [[Wikipedia:Achievement (video gaming)|achievement]] concerning the HK protocol pacifist package.{{WebCite|url=https://steamcommunity.com/games/208580/announcements/detail/145589580847766181|text=Introducing and updated KOTOR II for Mac, Linux, and Windows!|author=Aspyr-Blair|date=2015-07-21|work=[http://store.steampowered.com Steam]|archivedate=20180802144022}} The player receives the achievement, called "Short Circuit," after installing the package.{{WebCite|url=https://steamcommunity.com/stats/208580/achievements|text=Global Gameplay Stats|work=[http://store.steampowered.com Steam]|archivedate=20180126115317}} ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=HK-Pazifistenpaket#legends }} [[Category:Droid parts]]
